# Introduction

- Today we are going to look at one of the most powerful parables
- The Parable of the Talents teaches us about stewardship
- How we use what God has given us while we wait for His return

# The Story

- A master is about to go on a long journey
- He calls three servants and entrusts his wealth to them
- To the first: five talents. To the second: two. To the third: one
- The master gives each servant according to his ability

# The First Two Servants

- The servant with five talents went immediately to work
- He traded and invested, earning five more talents
- The servant with two talents doubled his master's investment
- These two servants were faithful — they used their gifts wisely

# The Third Servant

- The servant who received one talent was afraid
- He worried about losing the talent
- He dug a hole and hid his master's money
- He did nothing — paralyzed by fear

# The Master Returns

- After a long time the master returned and called his servants
- The first servant presented ten talents — master was overjoyed
- "Well done, good and faithful servant!"
- The second servant also received the same high praise

# Lessons for Us

- God has given each of us gifts — not all the same, but all meaningful
- God does not compare us to each other
- He compares us to what He gave us
- Fear is not an excuse for inaction

slide: 8
title: "Conclusion"

# Conclusion

- God is calling us to use our gifts for His kingdom
- The question is not "Do I have enough?"
- The question is "Am I using what I have?"
- Like faithful servants, let us bring back more than He gave us
